mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate decline observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue streams and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or retail sales growth decouples from GDP without equity impact.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints, triggering short-term equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production contractions in late-cycle regimes, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or labor market improvements do not translate to equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which directly boost related corporate earnings and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index above 101.0 during a post-shock recovery phase indicates rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, triggering positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO exceeds 101.0 in recovery regimes, or the signal fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio in walk-forward simulations.
